input.source:
def f(:
    pass

class Foo:
    pass

class Foo(Bar):
    pass

def f(x):
    return isinstance(x, int)

def s(el):
    el.addEventListener('click', f)

def h(req):
    return JSONResponse({})

from honest_type import vocabulary
v = vocabulary({'a': {'x'}, 'b': {'x'}})

from honest_type import vocabulary, composed
v = vocabulary({'a':{'x'}}, composed_types=[composed('c', requires={'nope':'x'}, captures='a')])

from honest_type import chain
p = chain()

from honest_type import vocabulary, predicate
v=vocabulary({'n': predicate(lambda s: int(s)>0)})

from honest_type import vocabulary, predicate
v=vocabulary({'a': predicate(f)})

from honest_type import chain, link, vocabulary
v = vocabulary({'a': {'x'}})
@link(accepts=v, emits=v)
def good(m): return m
def bad(m): return m
p = chain(good, bad)

from honest_type import chain, link, vocabulary
vin = vocabulary({'a': {'x'}})
vout = vocabulary({'b': {'y'}})
@link(accepts=vin, emits=vin)
def first(m): return m
@link(accepts=vout, emits=vout)
def second(m): return m
p = chain(first, second)

from honest_type import vocabulary, binding, link
v=vocabulary({'a':{'x'},'b':{'y'}})
bd=binding({'a':'slotA'})
@link(accepts=v, binds=bd)
def f(m): return m

from honest_type import vocabulary, binding, link
v=vocabulary({'a':{'x'}})
bd=binding({'a':'slotA','ghost':'slotG'})
@link(accepts=v, binds=bd)
def f(m): return m

from honest_type import vocabulary, binding, link
v=vocabulary({'sender':{'u1','u2'},'receiver':{'u1','u2'}})
bd=binding({'sender':'from','receiver':'to'})
@link(accepts=v, binds=bd)
def f(m): return m

from honest_type import link, vocabulary, binding
vin=vocabulary({'a':{'x'}})
vout=vocabulary({'a':{'x'},'b':{'y'}})
bd=binding({'a':'slotA','b':'slotB'})
@link(accepts=vin, emits=vout, binds=bd)
def f(m):
    return m

from honest_type import state_machine, vocabulary
m = state_machine(
  states=vocabulary({'s': {'pending','paid'}}),
  events=vocabulary({'e': {'pay'}}),
  initial='pending',
  transitions={('PENDING','pay'):'paid'})

from honest_type import state_machine, vocabulary
m = state_machine(
  states=vocabulary({'s': {'pending','paid'}}),
  events=vocabulary({'e': {'pay'}}),
  initial='pending',
  transitions={('pending','PAY'):'paid'})

from honest_type import state_machine, vocabulary
m = state_machine(
  states=vocabulary({'s': {'pending','paid','orphan'}}),
  events=vocabulary({'e': {'pay'}}),
  initial='pending',
  transitions={('pending','pay'):'paid'})

from honest_type import state_machine, vocabulary
m = state_machine(
  states=vocabulary({'s': {'pending','paid'}}),
  events=vocabulary({'e': {'pay'}}),
  initial='START',
  transitions={('pending','pay'):'paid'})

from honest_type import state_machine, vocabulary
m = state_machine(
  states=vocabulary({'s': {'pending','paid'}}),
  events=vocabulary({'e': {'pay'}}),
  initial='pending',
  transitions={('pending','pay'):'paid'})

def add(a, b):
    return a + b
class Widget {}

diagnostics.message: Class 'Widget' has no declared base. Honest Code permits a JavaScript class only as a subclass of Error. Use a plain object for data or a pure function.

input.source: class Widget extends Gadget {}

diagnostics.message: Class 'Widget' inherits from 'Gadget'. Use composition over inheritance.

input.source: class MyError extends Error {}
el.addEventListener('click', h);

diagnostics.message: Lifecycle hook 'addEventListener'. Use HTMX attributes or server-rendered HTML.

input.source: el.appendChild(node);
const t = typeof x === 'string';

diagnostics.message: typeof check in business logic. Consider a vocabulary declaration instead.
if/else-if chain dispatches on value — use dict lookup. See honest-code-principles.md §3.

input.source: function f() { return fetch(u); }

diagnostics.message: 'fetch' performs I/O or non-deterministic work inside a non-boundary function. Mark the function '// honest: boundary', or it cannot be verified for purity.

input.source:
// honest: boundary
function f() { return fetch(u); }
function f() { try { g(); } catch (e) { h(); } }

diagnostics.message: Function 'f' catches an exception in business logic. Let it raise and catch at the boundary (a '// honest: boundary' function), or return a fault as data.

input.source: function f() { try { g(); } finally { h(); } }
const c = new WeakMap();

diagnostics.message: Cache 'WeakMap' detected without profiling evidence. Profile the path it optimises, or dismiss with '// honest: ignore HC-P006'.

input.source: const m = new Map();

diagnostics.message: Inner function captures ['x'] via closure and mutates it. Closures may not carry mutable state — use pure parameters or move state into persist.
feature_state references 'ghost', which is not a declared flag in FEATURES.
Handler table 'HANDLERS' is missing an entry for these states of 'new_checkout': ['off'].

expected.message: Class 'Foo' has no declared base. Honest Code permits class definitions only as subclasses of TypedDict, Protocol, ABC, or a declared Exception. Use a TypedDict for data shapes or a pure function.

cases.description: A file-level disable that suppresses nothing is dead and must be removed

diagnostics.message: Suppression of HC-P001 matched no diagnostic. A dead directive silently covers whatever this file grows next — delete it. See honest-check-architecture.md §7.4.

cases.description: An inline ignore on a line with no violation is dead

A suppression without a reason is an error, even when it is live

diagnostics.message: Suppression carries no reason. Write '# honest: disable RULE: why' so the next reader can judge it. See honest-check-architecture.md §7.4.

The suppression rules cannot themselves be suppressed

cases.description: A second disable of an already-open rule suppresses nothing of its own

value_oracle_exempt.reason: An I/O boundary: reads the given paths and reports through on_error (warn/raise), returning None. Its effect is not a return value; verified by its own conformance.
